summaries.summary: --- title: Meta, Google lose $3 million social media case author: Lynn La url: https://calmatters.org/newsletter/meta-google-verdict/ hostname: calmatters.org description: One of many lawsuits against Meta and Google over social media addiction ended Wednesday in favor of the plaintiff suing the tech giants. sitename: CalMatters date: 2026-03-26 categories: ['WhatMatters'] tags: ['Mental Health Reporting Initiative', 'What’s Working'] --- A California jury directed Google and Meta to cough up a total of $3 million Wednesday in a landmark verdict in favor of a young plaintiff whose family claimed that the companies knowingly made their social media platforms addictive to teens. As CalMatters’ Colin Lecher explains, the case is one of a spate of lawsuits that seek to hold the makers of sites such as YouTube and Facebook accountable for allegedly designing their products to be addictive, despite knowing the damage they could inflict on users’ mental health.

Several Southeast Asian nations are reviving mothballed nuclear plans and setting ambitious targets and nearly half of the region could, if they pursue those goals, have nuclear energy in the 2030s.
